mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a negative change in FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy rate is already near the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising readings in repeated out-of-sample tests, or if the sign of the effect flips in a rolling 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration, weaker income growth, and lower expected earnings, so when UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return after a qualifying UNRATE rise is indistinguishable from unconditional returns at conventional significance levels.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening household income and retail demand, which supports near-term revenue expectations and lifts equities over the next month; the signal is strongest when both series are rising together.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 130848.0 and RSAFS was 344688.0 as of the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint state of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone in explaining 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns are not concentrated in the joint-positive state.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in INDPRO reflects improving industrial supply conditions and stronger cyclical growth, so positive monthly changes in industrial production predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in INDPRO fail to precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns over multiple cycles, or if the signal is lost after accounting for standard lag structure.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises from depressed levels, it signals a housing-cycle rebound that improves construction activity, durable demand, and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if crossovers above the 6-month average do not lead to positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is no better than 50% in triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated CPIAUCSL combined with a non-easing FEDFUNDS rate compresses real income and raises policy uncertainty, so when inflation is high and policy is not cut, S&P 500 returns are negative over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL above its 12-month trend and FEDFUNDS not falling
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 215.791 and FEDFUNDS was 0.12% in the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation/non-easing state does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than other states, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a severe unemployment regime, further deterioration in UNRATE transmits quickly to earnings expectations and credit risk, so increases in unemployment predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE >= 9.5% and PAYEMS falling for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 10.2% and PAYEMS was 130848.0 as of the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if recession-triggered observations do not show negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if positive returns dominate despite sustained unemployment stress and falling payrolls.

mechanism_narrative: A contemporaneous rebound in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates a synchronized acceleration in aggregate output and final demand, which should lift equities over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 13014.0 and RSAFS was 344688.0 in the snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the combined signal fails to outperform either series individually.
